item.title: Solution 1/2 of nope cloud
item.content:encoded: That cloud of nope, I do have a solution to thought it would need a air wing and napalm. A issue with that much force is that GOC would be more willing to do such thought. (To be honest) A solution of fire and venom like a blood agent could be more convenient and efficient.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this. I love just about all the Sarkic stuff, and I dig the way you tied in one of the Archons. I can only imagine the horror of watching an entire Swarm coming over the horizon. "That there's a whole cloud of nope."

My only issue is probably semantic, but I figured I'd mention it. I'm not sure if the Review Board is your invention or someone else's, but the way it's labeled makes me stumble a bit in the reading flow. "Review Board of Keter Classed Objects" would read easier (to me, at least) as "The Keter Classed Objects Review Board". I believe the syntax rule is:

 <thing being reviewed> <reviewing body> of <institution>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read it all, no skimming. +1

Still not a fan of this wizard lore, but fair is fair, an upvote in exchange for the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some of your suggested changes, but to address some things.

+ show block
– hide block

Akkadian doesn't make any sense, unless you're trying to reference the scorpion men of Mesopotamian mythology.

Ya got it! Never thought anyone would notice why they speak Akkadian. I do have plans to write about the scorpion men in a future article, and my roach looking dudes definitely encounter them regularly.

What is the point of this footnote?

That they would use other materials besides wood? It is to essentially answer anyone's question if they strictly use wood or what if there aren't any wood. Well, I did edited the footnote slightly to say it is more of a preference regarding why they use wood.

You say "unknown deity" but tell us her name in the next paragraph (unless this is a different god?).

By unknown, I mean the Foundation never heard of this deity anywhere else. Knowing a deities name doesn't mean you know much about it. Therefore, it is unknown to the Foundation because they don't know many details beside what the SCP says about it.

Anyway, thank you commenting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the bug people, but it's got a few errors and could be revised.

+ show block
– hide block

Instances have spoken Akkadian, English, Sarkite, and Deavite.

"Sarkite" isn't a language, the Sarkics (who don't call themselves that, btw) speak Adytite. Also, "Daevite" is misspelled. Your choice of English seems to be just so the Foundation can communicate more easily with them, and Akkadian doesn't make any sense, unless you're trying to reference the scorpion men of Mesopotamian mythology.

in the Białowieża Forest at Poland

Since when was Poland considered part of Western Europe? Also, in Poland is more appropriate.

Instances would use alternative materials in construction.

What is the point of this footnote?

These effigies are made to resemble an unknown deity worshiped by -1 instances, and are designated as SCP-4067-2. When it is built, -1 instances will gather any near-by humans or animals, and then in a ritualistic fashion have the bodies of subjects expand and merge with the -2 instance while chanting. Once a -2 is completely covered in the flesh of subjects, it will begin to animate and slowly crawl its vicinity in search of further subjects to consume, including -1 instances.

You say "unknown deity" but tell us her name in the next paragraph (unless this is a different god?).
It's more professional to write out the complete designation (i.e. SCP-4067-1 instances)
How do they "have the bodies of subjects expand and merge"? Is the chanting connected? Is it a Sarkic ritual? It's not clear from the writing.

SCP-4067-1 instances have produced offerings at the events, such as carvings or sculptures, depicting their deity, and each depiction varies but is of an insect appearance. Usual depictions are a humanoid or more resembling an ordinary insect.

This would read better if it was split up into smaller sentences. For instance, you could split it after "depicting their deity". The last sentence makes no sense to me. Is it more humanoid or insectoid?

mummified remains were discovered of SCP-4067-1 instances

Would read more smoothly as "mummified remains of SCP-4067-1 instances were discovered.
And how many of them were found? Were they able to find out the age of the remains?

A scroll was also recovered and contained Daeva script

"A scroll containing Daeva script was also recovered."


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

Recently, reports have been submitted of an increase in size and frequency of SCP-4067 events and with two had occurred occurring in the same year. The additional event had occurred in Northern Africa. In response, we propose the an immediate update to the special containment procedures.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
im more partial to:

"For those of you who volunteered to be injected with homo sapien DNA…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "For those of you who volunteered to be injected with praying mantis DNA, I've got some good news and some bad news. The bad news is we're postponing the test indefinitely. The good news is we've got a much better test for you: fighting an army of Mantis Men. Pick up a rifle and follow the yellow line. You'll know when the test starts."

item.title: Re: Grammar
item.content:encoded:
I made the changes.

Edit: Did not see you update your post.

item.title: Grammar
item.content:encoded:
Interactions with -1 instances is are to be accomplished via automatic means.

The remains of one -2 instance in Foundation custody is are currently kept.

Dissection have has shown. Or plural Dissections if you want to keep the 'have'.

where the instance it is attach attached to attends.

an increase in size of SCP-4067 events and two had occur occurred in the same year.

We wait for you your response.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I am back with a new article. This was an old draft I originally planned to be my submission to the 4000 contest, but I shelved it. Recently, I decided to finish it and here it is now.

I would like to thank Reverend Fox for their critique.

Image Credits:

https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FireflyEffigy.jpg

https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ica_stones15.JPG

Keter Logo made by artist CalicoStonewolf: https://www.deviantart.com/calicostonewolf/art/SCP-Foundation-Classes-738951532
